    Chapter 2.2: Scheduler Design¶

    Learning Objectives¶

    By the end of this notebook, you will:

    1. Understand why the scheduler is the most critical component of an LLM serving system
    2. Implement and compare FCFS, priority-based, and SLO-aware scheduling algorithms
    3. Explain preemption strategies: swap-to-CPU vs recomputation, and when to use each
    4. Design admission control policies for limited GPU memory
    5. Analyze the fairness vs efficiency tradeoff in scheduler design
    6. Visualize scheduling decisions on timeline diagrams

    Prerequisites¶

    • Chapter 2.1: Request Lifecycle (understanding of prefill/decode phases)
    • Basic understanding of operating system scheduling concepts

    How to do the exercises:

    1. Google Colab (Recommended): Click the "Open in Colab" badge above — you get your own copy with free GPU.
    2. Local Jupyter: Clone the repo, run ./start.sh, then open notebooks at http://localhost:8888.
    3. Exercises: Look for cells marked with 🏋️ Exercise or Assignment. Fill in the TODO sections and run the cell to check your work.

    Tip: In Colab, the notebook is automatically copied to your Drive — your changes are saved there.

    1. Why Is the Scheduler the Brain of LLM Serving?¶

    In traditional web servers, a request uses CPU for a few milliseconds and returns. In LLM serving, a single request can occupy gigabytes of GPU memory (KV-cache) and require seconds of continuous GPU computation. The scheduler must answer hard questions every few milliseconds:

    • Which requests get GPU time NOW? (out of potentially hundreds waiting)
    • Should we start a new request or continue existing ones? (prefill vs decode priority)
    • Is there enough memory for this new request's KV-cache?
    • Should we evict a running request to make room for a higher-priority one?
    • How do we meet SLO guarantees while maximizing throughput?

    The Scheduling Loop¶

    The scheduler runs in a tight loop, making decisions at every iteration:

    while True:
    # 1. Check for completed requests (hit EOS or max_tokens)
    # 2. Free memory from completed requests
    # 3. Check if waiting requests can be admitted (memory check)
    # 4. Decide the batch for this iteration
    # 5. Execute the batch on GPU
    # 6. Process results (sampling, stopping criteria)
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    ## Figure: Scheduler Three-Queue Architecture
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as patches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 8))
ax.set_xlim(0, 18)
ax.set_ylim(0, 10)
ax.axis('off')
ax.set_title('Scheduler Queue Architecture: Waiting, Running, Swapped', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')

BLUE, GREEN, ORANGE, RED, PURPLE = '#4A90D9', '#27AE60', '#F39C12', '#E74C3C', '#8E44AD'

# Waiting Queue
ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((1, 5.5), 4, 3, boxstyle="round,pad=0.2",
             facecolor=ORANGE, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2))
ax.text(3, 8.2, 'WAITING Queue', ha='center', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='white')
for i in range(4):
    ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1.5, 7.5 - i*0.6), 3, 0.45, facecolor='white', alpha=0.8, edgecolor=ORANGE))
    ax.text(3, 7.72 - i*0.6, f'Request {i+5}', ha='center', fontsize=8)
ax.text(3, 5.0, 'Queued, not yet\nscheduled', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic')

# Running Queue
ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((7, 5.5), 4, 3, boxstyle="round,pad=0.2",
             facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2))
ax.text(9, 8.2, 'RUNNING Queue', ha='center', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='white')
for i in range(3):
    ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((7.5, 7.5 - i*0.6), 3, 0.45, facecolor='white', alpha=0.8, edgecolor=GREEN))
    ax.text(9, 7.72 - i*0.6, f'Request {i} (decoding)', ha='center', fontsize=8)
ax.text(9, 5.0, 'Active on GPU,\nKV-cache in GPU memory', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic')

# Swapped Queue
ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((13, 5.5), 4, 3, boxstyle="round,pad=0.2",
             facecolor=RED, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2))
ax.text(15, 8.2, 'SWAPPED Queue', ha='center', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='white')
for i in range(2):
    ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((13.5, 7.5 - i*0.6), 3, 0.45, facecolor='white', alpha=0.8, edgecolor=RED))
    ax.text(15, 7.72 - i*0.6, f'Request {i+3} (preempted)', ha='center', fontsize=8)
ax.text(15, 5.0, 'KV-cache saved\nto CPU memory', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic')

# Arrows
# Waiting -> Running (schedule)
ax.annotate('', xy=(7, 7.0), xytext=(5, 7.0),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2.5, color=GREEN))
ax.text(6, 7.4, 'Schedule\n(prefill)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=GREEN)

# Running -> Swapped (preempt)
ax.annotate('', xy=(13, 6.5), xytext=(11, 6.5),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2.5, color=RED))
ax.text(12, 6.0, 'Preempt\n(swap out)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=RED)

# Swapped -> Running (resume)
ax.annotate('', xy=(11, 7.5), xytext=(13, 7.5),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2.5, color=BLUE))
ax.text(12, 8.0, 'Resume\n(swap in)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=BLUE)

# Running -> Complete
ax.annotate('', xy=(9, 5.0), xytext=(9, 5.4),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2, color=PURPLE))
ax.text(9, 4.5, 'Complete\n(free KV-cache)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=PURPLE)

# GPU memory bar at bottom
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1, 2), 16, 1.5, facecolor='#ECF0F1', edgecolor='black', linewidth=1.5))
ax.text(9, 3.2, 'GPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1.2, 2.2), 5, 0.5, facecolor=BLUE, alpha=0.7))
ax.text(3.7, 2.45, 'Model Weights', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='white', fontweight='bold')
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((6.5, 2.2), 6, 0.5, facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.7))
ax.text(9.5, 2.45, 'KV-Cache (Running Requests)', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='white', fontweight='bold')
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((12.8, 2.2), 4, 0.5, facecolor='lightgray', alpha=0.5))
ax.text(14.8, 2.45, 'Free', ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print("The scheduler manages three queues: Waiting (pending), Running (active on GPU), Swapped (preempted to CPU).")
print("When GPU memory is low, running requests can be preempted (swapped) to make room for new ones.")
    ## Figure: Scheduler Three-Queue Architecture import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.patches as patches fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 8)) ax.set_xlim(0, 18) ax.set_ylim(0, 10) ax.axis('off') ax.set_title('Scheduler Queue Architecture: Waiting, Running, Swapped', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold') BLUE, GREEN, ORANGE, RED, PURPLE = '#4A90D9', '#27AE60', '#F39C12', '#E74C3C', '#8E44AD' # Waiting Queue ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((1, 5.5), 4, 3, boxstyle="round,pad=0.2", facecolor=ORANGE, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2)) ax.text(3, 8.2, 'WAITING Queue', ha='center', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='white') for i in range(4): ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1.5, 7.5 - i*0.6), 3, 0.45, facecolor='white', alpha=0.8, edgecolor=ORANGE)) ax.text(3, 7.72 - i*0.6, f'Request {i+5}', ha='center', fontsize=8) ax.text(3, 5.0, 'Queued, not yet\nscheduled', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic') # Running Queue ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((7, 5.5), 4, 3, boxstyle="round,pad=0.2", facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2)) ax.text(9, 8.2, 'RUNNING Queue', ha='center', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='white') for i in range(3): ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((7.5, 7.5 - i*0.6), 3, 0.45, facecolor='white', alpha=0.8, edgecolor=GREEN)) ax.text(9, 7.72 - i*0.6, f'Request {i} (decoding)', ha='center', fontsize=8) ax.text(9, 5.0, 'Active on GPU,\nKV-cache in GPU memory', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic') # Swapped Queue ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((13, 5.5), 4, 3, boxstyle="round,pad=0.2", facecolor=RED, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2)) ax.text(15, 8.2, 'SWAPPED Queue', ha='center', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color='white') for i in range(2): ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((13.5, 7.5 - i*0.6), 3, 0.45, facecolor='white', alpha=0.8, edgecolor=RED)) ax.text(15, 7.72 - i*0.6, f'Request {i+3} (preempted)', ha='center', fontsize=8) ax.text(15, 5.0, 'KV-cache saved\nto CPU memory', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic') # Arrows # Waiting -> Running (schedule) ax.annotate('', xy=(7, 7.0), xytext=(5, 7.0), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2.5, color=GREEN)) ax.text(6, 7.4, 'Schedule\n(prefill)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=GREEN) # Running -> Swapped (preempt) ax.annotate('', xy=(13, 6.5), xytext=(11, 6.5), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2.5, color=RED)) ax.text(12, 6.0, 'Preempt\n(swap out)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=RED) # Swapped -> Running (resume) ax.annotate('', xy=(11, 7.5), xytext=(13, 7.5), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2.5, color=BLUE)) ax.text(12, 8.0, 'Resume\n(swap in)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=BLUE) # Running -> Complete ax.annotate('', xy=(9, 5.0), xytext=(9, 5.4), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=2, color=PURPLE)) ax.text(9, 4.5, 'Complete\n(free KV-cache)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=PURPLE) # GPU memory bar at bottom ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1, 2), 16, 1.5, facecolor='#ECF0F1', edgecolor='black', linewidth=1.5)) ax.text(9, 3.2, 'GPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold') ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1.2, 2.2), 5, 0.5, facecolor=BLUE, alpha=0.7)) ax.text(3.7, 2.45, 'Model Weights', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='white', fontweight='bold') ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((6.5, 2.2), 6, 0.5, facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.7)) ax.text(9.5, 2.45, 'KV-Cache (Running Requests)', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='white', fontweight='bold') ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((12.8, 2.2), 4, 0.5, facecolor='lightgray', alpha=0.5)) ax.text(14.8, 2.45, 'Free', ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold') plt.tight_layout() plt.show() print("The scheduler manages three queues: Waiting (pending), Running (active on GPU), Swapped (preempted to CPU).") print("When GPU memory is low, running requests can be preempted (swapped) to make room for new ones.")
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Tuple
from enum import Enum
import heapq
import random
import time
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

class RequestState(Enum):
    WAITING = "waiting"      # In queue, waiting to be scheduled
    RUNNING = "running"      # Currently being processed on GPU
    PREEMPTED = "preempted"  # Evicted from GPU, waiting to resume
    COMPLETED = "completed"  # Finished generation

@dataclass
class SchedulerRequest:
    """A request from the scheduler's perspective."""
    request_id: str
    arrival_time: float
    prompt_tokens: int
    max_gen_tokens: int
    priority: int = 0  # Higher = more important
    slo_ttft_ms: float = 5000  # TTFT SLO in milliseconds
    slo_tpot_ms: float = 100   # TPOT SLO in milliseconds
    
    # Runtime state
    state: RequestState = RequestState.WAITING
    generated_tokens: int = 0
    kv_cache_blocks: int = 0  # Number of KV-cache blocks allocated
    
    # Timing
    first_scheduled_time: Optional[float] = None
    first_token_time: Optional[float] = None
    completion_time: Optional[float] = None
    total_preemptions: int = 0
    
    # For timeline visualization
    timeline: List[Tuple[float, float, str]] = field(default_factory=list)  # (start, end, state)
    
    @property
    def remaining_tokens(self):
        return self.max_gen_tokens - self.generated_tokens
    
    @property
    def total_tokens(self):
        return self.prompt_tokens + self.generated_tokens
    
    def __lt__(self, other):
        return self.arrival_time < other.arrival_time

print("Request and state models defined.")
print(f"States: {[s.value for s in RequestState]}")
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.patches as mpatches import numpy as np from dataclasses import dataclass, field from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Tuple from enum import Enum import heapq import random import time import warnings warnings.filterwarnings('ignore') class RequestState(Enum): WAITING = "waiting" # In queue, waiting to be scheduled RUNNING = "running" # Currently being processed on GPU PREEMPTED = "preempted" # Evicted from GPU, waiting to resume COMPLETED = "completed" # Finished generation @dataclass class SchedulerRequest: """A request from the scheduler's perspective.""" request_id: str arrival_time: float prompt_tokens: int max_gen_tokens: int priority: int = 0 # Higher = more important slo_ttft_ms: float = 5000 # TTFT SLO in milliseconds slo_tpot_ms: float = 100 # TPOT SLO in milliseconds # Runtime state state: RequestState = RequestState.WAITING generated_tokens: int = 0 kv_cache_blocks: int = 0 # Number of KV-cache blocks allocated # Timing first_scheduled_time: Optional[float] = None first_token_time: Optional[float] = None completion_time: Optional[float] = None total_preemptions: int = 0 # For timeline visualization timeline: List[Tuple[float, float, str]] = field(default_factory=list) # (start, end, state) @property def remaining_tokens(self): return self.max_gen_tokens - self.generated_tokens @property def total_tokens(self): return self.prompt_tokens + self.generated_tokens def __lt__(self, other): return self.arrival_time < other.arrival_time print("Request and state models defined.") print(f"States: {[s.value for s in RequestState]}")
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    ## Figure: Preemption Strategies -- Swap vs Recompute
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as patches

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 7))

BLUE, GREEN, ORANGE, RED = '#4A90D9', '#27AE60', '#F39C12', '#E74C3C'

# LEFT: Swap to CPU
ax = axes[0]
ax.set_xlim(0, 10)
ax.set_ylim(0, 10)
ax.axis('off')
ax.set_title('Strategy 1: Swap to CPU\n(Save KV-cache)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color=BLUE)

# GPU Memory
ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((0.5, 5), 4, 4.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.15",
             facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2))
ax.text(2.5, 9.2, 'GPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1, 7), 3, 1.5, facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black'))
ax.text(2.5, 7.75, 'KV-Cache\n(Request A)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='white')

# CPU Memory
ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((5.5, 5), 4, 4.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.15",
             facecolor=BLUE, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2))
ax.text(7.5, 9.2, 'CPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')

# Arrow: swap out
ax.annotate('', xy=(5.5, 7.5), xytext=(4.5, 7.5),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=3, color=RED))
ax.text(5, 8.2, 'Swap Out\n(PCIe transfer)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=RED)

# Saved on CPU
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((6, 7), 3, 1.5, facecolor=BLUE, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black'))
ax.text(7.5, 7.75, 'KV-Cache\n(saved copy)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='white')

# Arrow: swap in (later)
ax.annotate('', xy=(4.5, 5.8), xytext=(5.5, 5.8),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=3, color=GREEN))
ax.text(5, 5.2, 'Swap In\n(resume later)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=GREEN)

# Pros/cons
ax.text(5, 3.5, 'Pros: Fast resume (no recomputation)', fontsize=9, color=GREEN, fontweight='bold', ha='center')
ax.text(5, 2.5, 'Cons: PCIe bandwidth cost,\nCPU memory needed', fontsize=9, color=RED, ha='center')
ax.text(5, 1.5, 'Best for: Long sequences\n(recompute would be expensive)', fontsize=9, color=BLUE, ha='center')

# RIGHT: Recompute
ax = axes[1]
ax.set_xlim(0, 10)
ax.set_ylim(0, 10)
ax.axis('off')
ax.set_title('Strategy 2: Recompute\n(Discard KV-cache)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color=ORANGE)

# GPU Memory
ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((2, 5), 6, 4.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.15",
             facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2))
ax.text(5, 9.2, 'GPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')

# KV-cache being discarded
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((2.5, 7), 5, 1.5, facecolor=RED, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='red',
             linewidth=2, linestyle='--'))
ax.text(5, 7.75, 'KV-Cache DISCARDED', ha='center', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold', color=RED)
ax.plot([2.5, 7.5], [7, 8.5], 'r-', linewidth=3, alpha=0.5)
ax.plot([2.5, 7.5], [8.5, 7], 'r-', linewidth=3, alpha=0.5)

# Free space
ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((2.5, 5.3), 5, 1.2, facecolor='lightgreen', alpha=0.3, edgecolor=GREEN))
ax.text(5, 5.9, 'Memory FREED\nfor other requests', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=GREEN)

# Resume: recompute from prompt
ax.text(5, 3.5, 'Pros: No CPU memory needed,\nno transfer cost', fontsize=9, color=GREEN, fontweight='bold', ha='center')
ax.text(5, 2.5, 'Cons: Must re-run prefill\nwhen resuming', fontsize=9, color=RED, ha='center')
ax.text(5, 1.5, 'Best for: Short sequences\n(cheap to recompute)', fontsize=9, color=ORANGE, ha='center')

plt.suptitle('Preemption Strategies: How to Handle GPU Memory Pressure', fontsize=15, fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print("vLLM supports both strategies. Swap is default for sequences > 512 tokens.")
    ## Figure: Preemption Strategies -- Swap vs Recompute import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.patches as patches fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 7)) BLUE, GREEN, ORANGE, RED = '#4A90D9', '#27AE60', '#F39C12', '#E74C3C' # LEFT: Swap to CPU ax = axes[0] ax.set_xlim(0, 10) ax.set_ylim(0, 10) ax.axis('off') ax.set_title('Strategy 1: Swap to CPU\n(Save KV-cache)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color=BLUE) # GPU Memory ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((0.5, 5), 4, 4.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.15", facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2)) ax.text(2.5, 9.2, 'GPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold') ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((1, 7), 3, 1.5, facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')) ax.text(2.5, 7.75, 'KV-Cache\n(Request A)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='white') # CPU Memory ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((5.5, 5), 4, 4.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.15", facecolor=BLUE, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2)) ax.text(7.5, 9.2, 'CPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold') # Arrow: swap out ax.annotate('', xy=(5.5, 7.5), xytext=(4.5, 7.5), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=3, color=RED)) ax.text(5, 8.2, 'Swap Out\n(PCIe transfer)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=RED) # Saved on CPU ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((6, 7), 3, 1.5, facecolor=BLUE, alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black')) ax.text(7.5, 7.75, 'KV-Cache\n(saved copy)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='white') # Arrow: swap in (later) ax.annotate('', xy=(4.5, 5.8), xytext=(5.5, 5.8), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=3, color=GREEN)) ax.text(5, 5.2, 'Swap In\n(resume later)', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=GREEN) # Pros/cons ax.text(5, 3.5, 'Pros: Fast resume (no recomputation)', fontsize=9, color=GREEN, fontweight='bold', ha='center') ax.text(5, 2.5, 'Cons: PCIe bandwidth cost,\nCPU memory needed', fontsize=9, color=RED, ha='center') ax.text(5, 1.5, 'Best for: Long sequences\n(recompute would be expensive)', fontsize=9, color=BLUE, ha='center') # RIGHT: Recompute ax = axes[1] ax.set_xlim(0, 10) ax.set_ylim(0, 10) ax.axis('off') ax.set_title('Strategy 2: Recompute\n(Discard KV-cache)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', color=ORANGE) # GPU Memory ax.add_patch(patches.FancyBboxPatch((2, 5), 6, 4.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.15", facecolor=GREEN, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black', linewidth=2)) ax.text(5, 9.2, 'GPU Memory', ha='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold') # KV-cache being discarded ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((2.5, 7), 5, 1.5, facecolor=RED, alpha=0.3, edgecolor='red', linewidth=2, linestyle='--')) ax.text(5, 7.75, 'KV-Cache DISCARDED', ha='center', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold', color=RED) ax.plot([2.5, 7.5], [7, 8.5], 'r-', linewidth=3, alpha=0.5) ax.plot([2.5, 7.5], [8.5, 7], 'r-', linewidth=3, alpha=0.5) # Free space ax.add_patch(patches.Rectangle((2.5, 5.3), 5, 1.2, facecolor='lightgreen', alpha=0.3, edgecolor=GREEN)) ax.text(5, 5.9, 'Memory FREED\nfor other requests', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=GREEN) # Resume: recompute from prompt ax.text(5, 3.5, 'Pros: No CPU memory needed,\nno transfer cost', fontsize=9, color=GREEN, fontweight='bold', ha='center') ax.text(5, 2.5, 'Cons: Must re-run prefill\nwhen resuming', fontsize=9, color=RED, ha='center') ax.text(5, 1.5, 'Best for: Short sequences\n(cheap to recompute)', fontsize=9, color=ORANGE, ha='center') plt.suptitle('Preemption Strategies: How to Handle GPU Memory Pressure', fontsize=15, fontweight='bold', y=1.02) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() print("vLLM supports both strategies. Swap is default for sequences > 512 tokens.")

    2. FCFS (First Come First Served) Scheduler¶

    The simplest scheduling policy: serve requests in the order they arrive.

    Algorithm¶

    1. Maintain a FIFO queue of waiting requests
    2. Each iteration, admit as many requests from the queue as memory allows
    3. No preemption: once a request starts, it runs to completion

    Pros¶

    • Simple to implement and reason about
    • Fair in a strict ordering sense
    • No starvation

    Cons¶

    • Head-of-line blocking: A long request delays all subsequent ones
    • No SLO awareness: Cannot prioritize urgent requests
    • Poor memory utilization: Cannot preempt to reclaim memory
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    class FCFSScheduler:
    """First Come First Served scheduler."""
    
    def __init__(self, max_batch_size: int = 8, 
                 total_gpu_blocks: int = 1000,
                 block_size: int = 16):  # tokens per block
        self.max_batch_size = max_batch_size
        self.total_gpu_blocks = total_gpu_blocks
        self.block_size = block_size
        
        self.waiting_queue: List[SchedulerRequest] = []
        self.running_batch: List[SchedulerRequest] = []
        self.completed: List[SchedulerRequest] = []
        self.free_blocks = total_gpu_blocks
        self.current_time = 0.0
        
        # Metrics
        self.schedule_log: List[dict] = []
    
    def add_request(self, req: SchedulerRequest):
        """Add a new request to the waiting queue."""
        req.state = RequestState.WAITING
        self.waiting_queue.append(req)
    
    def _blocks_needed(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> int:
        """Calculate KV-cache blocks needed for a request."""
        total_tokens = req.prompt_tokens + req.max_gen_tokens
        return (total_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size
    
    def _blocks_used(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> int:
        """Blocks currently used by a running request."""
        return (req.total_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size
    
    def schedule(self) -> dict:
        """Make scheduling decision for current iteration.
        Returns dict with prefill and decode batches."""
        
        # Step 1: Check for completed requests
        newly_completed = []
        for req in self.running_batch:
            if req.generated_tokens >= req.max_gen_tokens:
                req.state = RequestState.COMPLETED
                req.completion_time = self.current_time
                self.free_blocks += req.kv_cache_blocks
                newly_completed.append(req)
        
        for req in newly_completed:
            self.running_batch.remove(req)
            self.completed.append(req)
        
        # Step 2: Admit new requests from waiting queue (FCFS order)
        prefill_batch = []
        while (self.waiting_queue and 
               len(self.running_batch) + len(prefill_batch) < self.max_batch_size):
            req = self.waiting_queue[0]
            blocks_needed = self._blocks_needed(req)
            
            if blocks_needed <= self.free_blocks:
                self.waiting_queue.pop(0)
                req.state = RequestState.RUNNING
                req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks_needed
                self.free_blocks -= blocks_needed
                req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time
                prefill_batch.append(req)
            else:
                break  # FCFS: can't skip ahead
        
        # Step 3: Build decode batch (all running requests)
        decode_batch = list(self.running_batch)
        
        # Step 4: Move prefill requests to running
        self.running_batch.extend(prefill_batch)
        
        decision = {
            'time': self.current_time,
            'prefill': [r.request_id for r in prefill_batch],
            'decode': [r.request_id for r in decode_batch],
            'completed': [r.request_id for r in newly_completed],
            'waiting': len(self.waiting_queue),
            'free_blocks': self.free_blocks,
        }
        self.schedule_log.append(decision)
        return decision
    
    def step(self):
        """Simulate one iteration: schedule + execute."""
        decision = self.schedule()
        
        # Simulate execution: each running request generates one token
        for req in self.running_batch:
            req.generated_tokens += 1
            if req.generated_tokens == 1:
                req.first_token_time = self.current_time
        
        self.current_time += 0.025  # 25ms per iteration
        return decision

# Simulate FCFS scheduling
fcfs = FCFSScheduler(max_batch_size=4, total_gpu_blocks=200, block_size=16)

# Create requests with varying sizes
requests_fcfs = [
    SchedulerRequest("A", 0.0, prompt_tokens=64, max_gen_tokens=20),
    SchedulerRequest("B", 0.01, prompt_tokens=128, max_gen_tokens=50),
    SchedulerRequest("C", 0.02, prompt_tokens=32, max_gen_tokens=10),
    SchedulerRequest("D", 0.03, prompt_tokens=256, max_gen_tokens=30),
    SchedulerRequest("E", 0.05, prompt_tokens=64, max_gen_tokens=15),
    SchedulerRequest("F", 0.1, prompt_tokens=128, max_gen_tokens=25),
]

for req in requests_fcfs:
    fcfs.add_request(req)

# Run simulation
print("=== FCFS Scheduling Simulation ===")
for i in range(80):
    decision = fcfs.step()
    if decision['prefill'] or decision['completed']:
        print(f"  t={decision['time']:.3f}s: prefill={decision['prefill']}, "
              f"decode={decision['decode']}, completed={decision['completed']}, "
              f"waiting={decision['waiting']}")
    if not fcfs.running_batch and not fcfs.waiting_queue:
        break

print(f"\n=== Results ===")
for req in fcfs.completed:
    ttft = (req.first_token_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000
    total = (req.completion_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000
    print(f"  Req {req.request_id}: TTFT={ttft:.0f}ms, Total={total:.0f}ms, "
          f"Tokens={req.generated_tokens}")
    class FCFSScheduler: """First Come First Served scheduler.""" def __init__(self, max_batch_size: int = 8, total_gpu_blocks: int = 1000, block_size: int = 16): # tokens per block self.max_batch_size = max_batch_size self.total_gpu_blocks = total_gpu_blocks self.block_size = block_size self.waiting_queue: List[SchedulerRequest] = [] self.running_batch: List[SchedulerRequest] = [] self.completed: List[SchedulerRequest] = [] self.free_blocks = total_gpu_blocks self.current_time = 0.0 # Metrics self.schedule_log: List[dict] = [] def add_request(self, req: SchedulerRequest): """Add a new request to the waiting queue.""" req.state = RequestState.WAITING self.waiting_queue.append(req) def _blocks_needed(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> int: """Calculate KV-cache blocks needed for a request.""" total_tokens = req.prompt_tokens + req.max_gen_tokens return (total_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size def _blocks_used(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> int: """Blocks currently used by a running request.""" return (req.total_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size def schedule(self) -> dict: """Make scheduling decision for current iteration. Returns dict with prefill and decode batches.""" # Step 1: Check for completed requests newly_completed = [] for req in self.running_batch: if req.generated_tokens >= req.max_gen_tokens: req.state = RequestState.COMPLETED req.completion_time = self.current_time self.free_blocks += req.kv_cache_blocks newly_completed.append(req) for req in newly_completed: self.running_batch.remove(req) self.completed.append(req) # Step 2: Admit new requests from waiting queue (FCFS order) prefill_batch = [] while (self.waiting_queue and len(self.running_batch) + len(prefill_batch) < self.max_batch_size): req = self.waiting_queue[0] blocks_needed = self._blocks_needed(req) if blocks_needed <= self.free_blocks: self.waiting_queue.pop(0) req.state = RequestState.RUNNING req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks_needed self.free_blocks -= blocks_needed req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time prefill_batch.append(req) else: break # FCFS: can't skip ahead # Step 3: Build decode batch (all running requests) decode_batch = list(self.running_batch) # Step 4: Move prefill requests to running self.running_batch.extend(prefill_batch) decision = { 'time': self.current_time, 'prefill': [r.request_id for r in prefill_batch], 'decode': [r.request_id for r in decode_batch], 'completed': [r.request_id for r in newly_completed], 'waiting': len(self.waiting_queue), 'free_blocks': self.free_blocks, } self.schedule_log.append(decision) return decision def step(self): """Simulate one iteration: schedule + execute.""" decision = self.schedule() # Simulate execution: each running request generates one token for req in self.running_batch: req.generated_tokens += 1 if req.generated_tokens == 1: req.first_token_time = self.current_time self.current_time += 0.025 # 25ms per iteration return decision # Simulate FCFS scheduling fcfs = FCFSScheduler(max_batch_size=4, total_gpu_blocks=200, block_size=16) # Create requests with varying sizes requests_fcfs = [ SchedulerRequest("A", 0.0, prompt_tokens=64, max_gen_tokens=20), SchedulerRequest("B", 0.01, prompt_tokens=128, max_gen_tokens=50), SchedulerRequest("C", 0.02, prompt_tokens=32, max_gen_tokens=10), SchedulerRequest("D", 0.03, prompt_tokens=256, max_gen_tokens=30), SchedulerRequest("E", 0.05, prompt_tokens=64, max_gen_tokens=15), SchedulerRequest("F", 0.1, prompt_tokens=128, max_gen_tokens=25), ] for req in requests_fcfs: fcfs.add_request(req) # Run simulation print("=== FCFS Scheduling Simulation ===") for i in range(80): decision = fcfs.step() if decision['prefill'] or decision['completed']: print(f" t={decision['time']:.3f}s: prefill={decision['prefill']}, " f"decode={decision['decode']}, completed={decision['completed']}, " f"waiting={decision['waiting']}") if not fcfs.running_batch and not fcfs.waiting_queue: break print(f"\n=== Results ===") for req in fcfs.completed: ttft = (req.first_token_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000 total = (req.completion_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000 print(f" Req {req.request_id}: TTFT={ttft:.0f}ms, Total={total:.0f}ms, " f"Tokens={req.generated_tokens}")

    3. Priority-Based Scheduler¶

    In production, not all requests are equal. Some users pay more, some tasks are time-sensitive, some requests are about to miss their SLO deadlines.

    Priority Sources¶

    Priority Factor Example Weight
    User tier Premium vs free High
    SLO urgency Time until SLO violation Critical
    Request age Prevent starvation Medium
    Estimated cost Prefer short requests Low

    SLO-Aware Priority¶

    The most sophisticated approach calculates priority dynamically based on how close a request is to violating its SLO:

    $$\text{priority} = \frac{\text{time\_remaining\_before\_SLO\_violation}}{\text{estimated\_remaining\_work}}$$

    Lower ratio = higher urgency = should be scheduled first.

    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    class PriorityScheduler:
    """Priority-based scheduler with SLO awareness."""
    
    def __init__(self, max_batch_size: int = 8,
                 total_gpu_blocks: int = 1000,
                 block_size: int = 16):
        self.max_batch_size = max_batch_size
        self.total_gpu_blocks = total_gpu_blocks
        self.block_size = block_size
        
        self.waiting_queue: List[SchedulerRequest] = []
        self.running_batch: List[SchedulerRequest] = []
        self.completed: List[SchedulerRequest] = []
        self.free_blocks = total_gpu_blocks
        self.current_time = 0.0
        self.iteration_time = 0.025  # 25ms per step
        self.schedule_log = []
    
    def _calculate_priority(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> float:
        """Calculate dynamic priority score. Higher = should be scheduled sooner."""
        wait_time = self.current_time - req.arrival_time
        
        if req.state == RequestState.WAITING:
            # For waiting requests: urgency based on TTFT SLO
            slo_remaining = (req.slo_ttft_ms / 1000) - wait_time
            estimated_work = req.prompt_tokens * 0.0001  # prefill time estimate
            urgency = max(0.001, slo_remaining) / max(0.001, estimated_work)
            # Lower urgency ratio = more urgent = higher priority score
            slo_priority = 100.0 / max(0.01, urgency)
        else:
            slo_priority = 50.0  # Running requests get medium base priority
        
        # Combine with static priority and age
        age_bonus = wait_time * 10  # Prevent starvation
        return req.priority * 20 + slo_priority + age_bonus
    
    def _blocks_needed(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> int:
        total_tokens = req.prompt_tokens + req.max_gen_tokens
        return (total_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size
    
    def add_request(self, req: SchedulerRequest):
        req.state = RequestState.WAITING
        self.waiting_queue.append(req)
    
    def schedule(self) -> dict:
        # Step 1: Remove completed requests
        newly_completed = []
        for req in self.running_batch:
            if req.generated_tokens >= req.max_gen_tokens:
                req.state = RequestState.COMPLETED
                req.completion_time = self.current_time
                self.free_blocks += req.kv_cache_blocks
                newly_completed.append(req)
        
        for req in newly_completed:
            self.running_batch.remove(req)
            self.completed.append(req)
        
        # Step 2: Sort waiting queue by priority (highest first)
        self.waiting_queue.sort(key=lambda r: self._calculate_priority(r), reverse=True)
        
        # Step 3: Admit requests by priority
        prefill_batch = []
        still_waiting = []
        for req in self.waiting_queue:
            if len(self.running_batch) + len(prefill_batch) >= self.max_batch_size:
                still_waiting.append(req)
                continue
            
            blocks_needed = self._blocks_needed(req)
            if blocks_needed <= self.free_blocks:
                req.state = RequestState.RUNNING
                req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks_needed
                self.free_blocks -= blocks_needed
                req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time
                prefill_batch.append(req)
            else:
                still_waiting.append(req)
        
        self.waiting_queue = still_waiting
        decode_batch = list(self.running_batch)
        self.running_batch.extend(prefill_batch)
        
        decision = {
            'time': self.current_time,
            'prefill': [(r.request_id, f"pri={self._calculate_priority(r):.1f}") for r in prefill_batch],
            'decode': [r.request_id for r in decode_batch],
            'completed': [r.request_id for r in newly_completed],
            'waiting': len(self.waiting_queue),
        }
        self.schedule_log.append(decision)
        return decision
    
    def step(self):
        decision = self.schedule()
        for req in self.running_batch:
            req.generated_tokens += 1
            if req.generated_tokens == 1:
                req.first_token_time = self.current_time
        self.current_time += self.iteration_time
        return decision

# Simulate priority scheduling
priority_sched = PriorityScheduler(max_batch_size=3, total_gpu_blocks=200, block_size=16)

# Same requests but with different priorities and SLOs
requests_pri = [
    SchedulerRequest("A", 0.0, 64, 20, priority=1, slo_ttft_ms=500),    # Low priority, tight SLO
    SchedulerRequest("B", 0.01, 128, 50, priority=3, slo_ttft_ms=2000),  # High priority
    SchedulerRequest("C", 0.02, 32, 10, priority=1, slo_ttft_ms=5000),   # Low priority, loose SLO
    SchedulerRequest("D", 0.03, 256, 30, priority=5, slo_ttft_ms=1000),  # Highest priority
    SchedulerRequest("E", 0.05, 64, 15, priority=2, slo_ttft_ms=3000),   # Medium priority
    SchedulerRequest("F", 0.1, 128, 25, priority=4, slo_ttft_ms=1500),   # High priority, medium SLO
]

for req in requests_pri:
    priority_sched.add_request(req)

print("=== Priority Scheduling Simulation ===")
for i in range(80):
    decision = priority_sched.step()
    if decision['prefill'] or decision['completed']:
        print(f"  t={decision['time']:.3f}s: prefill={decision['prefill']}, "
              f"completed={decision['completed']}, waiting={decision['waiting']}")
    if not priority_sched.running_batch and not priority_sched.waiting_queue:
        break

print(f"\n=== Results ===")
for req in sorted(priority_sched.completed, key=lambda r: r.request_id):
    ttft = (req.first_token_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000
    total = (req.completion_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000
    slo_met = ttft <= req.slo_ttft_ms
    print(f"  Req {req.request_id} (pri={req.priority}): TTFT={ttft:.0f}ms "
          f"(SLO={req.slo_ttft_ms}ms, {'MET' if slo_met else 'VIOLATED'}), "
          f"Total={total:.0f}ms")
    class PriorityScheduler: """Priority-based scheduler with SLO awareness.""" def __init__(self, max_batch_size: int = 8, total_gpu_blocks: int = 1000, block_size: int = 16): self.max_batch_size = max_batch_size self.total_gpu_blocks = total_gpu_blocks self.block_size = block_size self.waiting_queue: List[SchedulerRequest] = [] self.running_batch: List[SchedulerRequest] = [] self.completed: List[SchedulerRequest] = [] self.free_blocks = total_gpu_blocks self.current_time = 0.0 self.iteration_time = 0.025 # 25ms per step self.schedule_log = [] def _calculate_priority(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> float: """Calculate dynamic priority score. Higher = should be scheduled sooner.""" wait_time = self.current_time - req.arrival_time if req.state == RequestState.WAITING: # For waiting requests: urgency based on TTFT SLO slo_remaining = (req.slo_ttft_ms / 1000) - wait_time estimated_work = req.prompt_tokens * 0.0001 # prefill time estimate urgency = max(0.001, slo_remaining) / max(0.001, estimated_work) # Lower urgency ratio = more urgent = higher priority score slo_priority = 100.0 / max(0.01, urgency) else: slo_priority = 50.0 # Running requests get medium base priority # Combine with static priority and age age_bonus = wait_time * 10 # Prevent starvation return req.priority * 20 + slo_priority + age_bonus def _blocks_needed(self, req: SchedulerRequest) -> int: total_tokens = req.prompt_tokens + req.max_gen_tokens return (total_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size def add_request(self, req: SchedulerRequest): req.state = RequestState.WAITING self.waiting_queue.append(req) def schedule(self) -> dict: # Step 1: Remove completed requests newly_completed = [] for req in self.running_batch: if req.generated_tokens >= req.max_gen_tokens: req.state = RequestState.COMPLETED req.completion_time = self.current_time self.free_blocks += req.kv_cache_blocks newly_completed.append(req) for req in newly_completed: self.running_batch.remove(req) self.completed.append(req) # Step 2: Sort waiting queue by priority (highest first) self.waiting_queue.sort(key=lambda r: self._calculate_priority(r), reverse=True) # Step 3: Admit requests by priority prefill_batch = [] still_waiting = [] for req in self.waiting_queue: if len(self.running_batch) + len(prefill_batch) >= self.max_batch_size: still_waiting.append(req) continue blocks_needed = self._blocks_needed(req) if blocks_needed <= self.free_blocks: req.state = RequestState.RUNNING req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks_needed self.free_blocks -= blocks_needed req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time prefill_batch.append(req) else: still_waiting.append(req) self.waiting_queue = still_waiting decode_batch = list(self.running_batch) self.running_batch.extend(prefill_batch) decision = { 'time': self.current_time, 'prefill': [(r.request_id, f"pri={self._calculate_priority(r):.1f}") for r in prefill_batch], 'decode': [r.request_id for r in decode_batch], 'completed': [r.request_id for r in newly_completed], 'waiting': len(self.waiting_queue), } self.schedule_log.append(decision) return decision def step(self): decision = self.schedule() for req in self.running_batch: req.generated_tokens += 1 if req.generated_tokens == 1: req.first_token_time = self.current_time self.current_time += self.iteration_time return decision # Simulate priority scheduling priority_sched = PriorityScheduler(max_batch_size=3, total_gpu_blocks=200, block_size=16) # Same requests but with different priorities and SLOs requests_pri = [ SchedulerRequest("A", 0.0, 64, 20, priority=1, slo_ttft_ms=500), # Low priority, tight SLO SchedulerRequest("B", 0.01, 128, 50, priority=3, slo_ttft_ms=2000), # High priority SchedulerRequest("C", 0.02, 32, 10, priority=1, slo_ttft_ms=5000), # Low priority, loose SLO SchedulerRequest("D", 0.03, 256, 30, priority=5, slo_ttft_ms=1000), # Highest priority SchedulerRequest("E", 0.05, 64, 15, priority=2, slo_ttft_ms=3000), # Medium priority SchedulerRequest("F", 0.1, 128, 25, priority=4, slo_ttft_ms=1500), # High priority, medium SLO ] for req in requests_pri: priority_sched.add_request(req) print("=== Priority Scheduling Simulation ===") for i in range(80): decision = priority_sched.step() if decision['prefill'] or decision['completed']: print(f" t={decision['time']:.3f}s: prefill={decision['prefill']}, " f"completed={decision['completed']}, waiting={decision['waiting']}") if not priority_sched.running_batch and not priority_sched.waiting_queue: break print(f"\n=== Results ===") for req in sorted(priority_sched.completed, key=lambda r: r.request_id): ttft = (req.first_token_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000 total = (req.completion_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000 slo_met = ttft <= req.slo_ttft_ms print(f" Req {req.request_id} (pri={req.priority}): TTFT={ttft:.0f}ms " f"(SLO={req.slo_ttft_ms}ms, {'MET' if slo_met else 'VIOLATED'}), " f"Total={total:.0f}ms")

    4. Preemption: When and How to Preempt Running Requests¶

    Sometimes, even with priority scheduling, we need to stop a running request to make room for a more important one. This is called preemption.

    Why Preempt?¶

    1. A high-priority request arrives but GPU memory is full
    2. A request is about to violate its SLO and needs more GPU resources
    3. The batch is too large, causing unacceptable per-token latency for all requests

    Two Preemption Strategies¶

    Strategy 1: Swap to CPU¶

    • Save the KV-cache from GPU to CPU memory
    • When the request is re-scheduled, copy KV-cache back to GPU
    • Pro: No redundant computation
    • Con: PCIe bandwidth is limited; large KV-caches take time to transfer

    Strategy 2: Recomputation¶

    • Discard the KV-cache entirely
    • When re-scheduled, recompute from scratch (run prefill again)
    • Pro: No CPU memory needed, no transfer overhead
    • Con: Wastes the compute already done
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    # Visualize the two preemption strategies

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(16, 8))

# === Strategy 1: Swap to CPU ===
ax = axes[0]
ax.set_title('Preemption Strategy 1: Swap to CPU', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlim(0, 20)
ax.set_ylim(-0.5, 3.5)

# Request A: running, then preempted, then resumed
ax.barh(0, 5, left=0, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', label='Running (GPU)')
ax.barh(0, 1, left=5, height=0.6, color='#e74c3c', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', label='Swap Out (GPU->CPU)')
ax.barh(0, 6, left=6, height=0.6, color='#95a5a6', alpha=0.4, edgecolor='black', hatch='//', label='Preempted (CPU)')
ax.barh(0, 1, left=12, height=0.6, color='#f39c12', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', label='Swap In (CPU->GPU)')
ax.barh(0, 5, left=13, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
ax.text(2.5, 0, 'Req A', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white')
ax.text(9, 0, 'KV-cache on CPU', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, style='italic')
ax.text(15.5, 0, 'Resume A', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white')

# Request B: high priority, arrives and causes preemption
ax.barh(1, 6, left=6, height=0.6, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
ax.text(9, 1, 'Req B (HIGH PRIORITY)', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white')

# GPU Memory diagram
ax.barh(2.5, 5, left=0, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black')
ax.barh(2.5, 6, left=6, height=0.4, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black')
ax.barh(2.5, 5, left=13, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black')
ax.text(10, 3.2, 'GPU Memory Usage', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic')

ax.set_yticks([0, 1, 2.5])
ax.set_yticklabels(['Request A', 'Request B', 'GPU Memory'])
ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=8, ncol=2)
ax.set_xlabel('Time')

# === Strategy 2: Recomputation ===
ax = axes[1]
ax.set_title('Preemption Strategy 2: Recomputation', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlim(0, 20)
ax.set_ylim(-0.5, 3.5)

# Request A: running, preempted (KV discarded), then re-prefilled
ax.barh(0, 5, left=0, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
ax.barh(0, 6, left=5, height=0.6, color='#95a5a6', alpha=0.2, edgecolor='black', hatch='xx', label='KV-cache discarded')
ax.barh(0, 2, left=12, height=0.6, color='#e74c3c', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', label='Re-prefill')
ax.barh(0, 4, left=14, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
ax.text(2.5, 0, 'Req A', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white')
ax.text(8, 0, 'Waiting (no memory)', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, style='italic')
ax.text(13, 0, 'Re-prefill', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7, fontweight='bold')
ax.text(16, 0, 'Resume', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white')

# Request B
ax.barh(1, 6, left=5, height=0.6, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
ax.text(8, 1, 'Req B (HIGH PRIORITY)', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white')

ax.barh(2.5, 5, left=0, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black')
ax.barh(2.5, 6, left=5, height=0.4, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black')
ax.barh(2.5, 6, left=12, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black')
ax.text(10, 3.2, 'GPU Memory Usage', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic')
ax.annotate('Wasted compute!', xy=(3, 0.5), fontsize=10, color='red', fontweight='bold',
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='red'), xytext=(3, 1.8))

ax.set_yticks([0, 1, 2.5])
ax.set_yticklabels(['Request A', 'Request B', 'GPU Memory'])
ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=8, ncol=2)
ax.set_xlabel('Time')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    # Visualize the two preemption strategies fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(16, 8)) # === Strategy 1: Swap to CPU === ax = axes[0] ax.set_title('Preemption Strategy 1: Swap to CPU', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold') ax.set_xlim(0, 20) ax.set_ylim(-0.5, 3.5) # Request A: running, then preempted, then resumed ax.barh(0, 5, left=0, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', label='Running (GPU)') ax.barh(0, 1, left=5, height=0.6, color='#e74c3c', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', label='Swap Out (GPU->CPU)') ax.barh(0, 6, left=6, height=0.6, color='#95a5a6', alpha=0.4, edgecolor='black', hatch='//', label='Preempted (CPU)') ax.barh(0, 1, left=12, height=0.6, color='#f39c12', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', label='Swap In (CPU->GPU)') ax.barh(0, 5, left=13, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black') ax.text(2.5, 0, 'Req A', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white') ax.text(9, 0, 'KV-cache on CPU', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, style='italic') ax.text(15.5, 0, 'Resume A', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white') # Request B: high priority, arrives and causes preemption ax.barh(1, 6, left=6, height=0.6, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black') ax.text(9, 1, 'Req B (HIGH PRIORITY)', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white') # GPU Memory diagram ax.barh(2.5, 5, left=0, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black') ax.barh(2.5, 6, left=6, height=0.4, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black') ax.barh(2.5, 5, left=13, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black') ax.text(10, 3.2, 'GPU Memory Usage', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic') ax.set_yticks([0, 1, 2.5]) ax.set_yticklabels(['Request A', 'Request B', 'GPU Memory']) ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=8, ncol=2) ax.set_xlabel('Time') # === Strategy 2: Recomputation === ax = axes[1] ax.set_title('Preemption Strategy 2: Recomputation', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold') ax.set_xlim(0, 20) ax.set_ylim(-0.5, 3.5) # Request A: running, preempted (KV discarded), then re-prefilled ax.barh(0, 5, left=0, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black') ax.barh(0, 6, left=5, height=0.6, color='#95a5a6', alpha=0.2, edgecolor='black', hatch='xx', label='KV-cache discarded') ax.barh(0, 2, left=12, height=0.6, color='#e74c3c', alpha=0.6, edgecolor='black', label='Re-prefill') ax.barh(0, 4, left=14, height=0.6, color='#3498db', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black') ax.text(2.5, 0, 'Req A', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white') ax.text(8, 0, 'Waiting (no memory)', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, style='italic') ax.text(13, 0, 'Re-prefill', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7, fontweight='bold') ax.text(16, 0, 'Resume', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white') # Request B ax.barh(1, 6, left=5, height=0.6, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black') ax.text(8, 1, 'Req B (HIGH PRIORITY)', ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', color='white') ax.barh(2.5, 5, left=0, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black') ax.barh(2.5, 6, left=5, height=0.4, color='#e67e22', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black') ax.barh(2.5, 6, left=12, height=0.4, color='#3498db', alpha=0.3, edgecolor='black') ax.text(10, 3.2, 'GPU Memory Usage', ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic') ax.annotate('Wasted compute!', xy=(3, 0.5), fontsize=10, color='red', fontweight='bold', arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='red'), xytext=(3, 1.8)) ax.set_yticks([0, 1, 2.5]) ax.set_yticklabels(['Request A', 'Request B', 'GPU Memory']) ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=8, ncol=2) ax.set_xlabel('Time') plt.tight_layout() plt.show()
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    # When to choose Swap vs Recomputation

def analyze_preemption_strategy(kv_cache_size_mb: float, 
                                 prompt_tokens: int,
                                 pcie_bandwidth_gbps: float = 32.0,
                                 prefill_speed_tokens_per_sec: float = 10000):
    """Compare swap vs recomputation cost for a given request."""
    
    # Swap cost: transfer KV-cache to CPU and back
    swap_time_ms = 2 * (kv_cache_size_mb / 1024) / pcie_bandwidth_gbps * 1000  # round trip
    
    # Recomputation cost: re-run prefill
    recompute_time_ms = prompt_tokens / prefill_speed_tokens_per_sec * 1000
    
    return {
        'kv_cache_mb': kv_cache_size_mb,
        'prompt_tokens': prompt_tokens,
        'swap_time_ms': swap_time_ms,
        'recompute_time_ms': recompute_time_ms,
        'recommendation': 'swap' if swap_time_ms < recompute_time_ms else 'recompute',
    }

# Analyze for different scenarios
print("=== Preemption Strategy Analysis ===")
print(f"{'KV-Cache (MB)':>14} {'Prompt Tokens':>14} {'Swap (ms)':>10} {'Recompute (ms)':>15} {'Recommendation':>15}")
print("-" * 70)

scenarios = [
    (10, 128),    # Small cache, short prompt
    (50, 512),    # Medium cache, medium prompt
    (200, 2048),  # Large cache, long prompt
    (500, 4096),  # Very large cache, very long prompt
    (1000, 8192), # Huge cache, huge prompt
    (5, 1024),    # Small cache but long prompt (recompute wins)
    (500, 256),   # Large cache but short prompt (swap might lose)
]

swap_times = []
recomp_times = []

for kv_mb, prompt_tok in scenarios:
    result = analyze_preemption_strategy(kv_mb, prompt_tok)
    swap_times.append(result['swap_time_ms'])
    recomp_times.append(result['recompute_time_ms'])
    print(f"{kv_mb:>14} {prompt_tok:>14} {result['swap_time_ms']:>10.2f} "
          f"{result['recompute_time_ms']:>15.2f} {result['recommendation']:>15}")
    # When to choose Swap vs Recomputation def analyze_preemption_strategy(kv_cache_size_mb: float, prompt_tokens: int, pcie_bandwidth_gbps: float = 32.0, prefill_speed_tokens_per_sec: float = 10000): """Compare swap vs recomputation cost for a given request.""" # Swap cost: transfer KV-cache to CPU and back swap_time_ms = 2 * (kv_cache_size_mb / 1024) / pcie_bandwidth_gbps * 1000 # round trip # Recomputation cost: re-run prefill recompute_time_ms = prompt_tokens / prefill_speed_tokens_per_sec * 1000 return { 'kv_cache_mb': kv_cache_size_mb, 'prompt_tokens': prompt_tokens, 'swap_time_ms': swap_time_ms, 'recompute_time_ms': recompute_time_ms, 'recommendation': 'swap' if swap_time_ms < recompute_time_ms else 'recompute', } # Analyze for different scenarios print("=== Preemption Strategy Analysis ===") print(f"{'KV-Cache (MB)':>14} {'Prompt Tokens':>14} {'Swap (ms)':>10} {'Recompute (ms)':>15} {'Recommendation':>15}") print("-" * 70) scenarios = [ (10, 128), # Small cache, short prompt (50, 512), # Medium cache, medium prompt (200, 2048), # Large cache, long prompt (500, 4096), # Very large cache, very long prompt (1000, 8192), # Huge cache, huge prompt (5, 1024), # Small cache but long prompt (recompute wins) (500, 256), # Large cache but short prompt (swap might lose) ] swap_times = [] recomp_times = [] for kv_mb, prompt_tok in scenarios: result = analyze_preemption_strategy(kv_mb, prompt_tok) swap_times.append(result['swap_time_ms']) recomp_times.append(result['recompute_time_ms']) print(f"{kv_mb:>14} {prompt_tok:>14} {result['swap_time_ms']:>10.2f} " f"{result['recompute_time_ms']:>15.2f} {result['recommendation']:>15}")
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    # Visualize the crossover point

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

kv_sizes = np.linspace(1, 1000, 100)
prompt_lens = [128, 512, 2048, 4096]
colors = ['#3498db', '#2ecc71', '#e74c3c', '#9b59b6']

# Swap time is only dependent on KV-cache size
swap_time = 2 * (kv_sizes / 1024) / 32.0 * 1000  # PCIe 4.0 x16
ax.plot(kv_sizes, swap_time, 'k--', linewidth=2, label='Swap (PCIe 4.0 x16)')

for prompt_len, color in zip(prompt_lens, colors):
    recomp_time = np.full_like(kv_sizes, prompt_len / 10000 * 1000)
    ax.axhline(y=prompt_len / 10000 * 1000, color=color, alpha=0.7, linestyle='-',
               label=f'Recompute ({prompt_len} tokens)')

ax.set_xlabel('KV-Cache Size (MB)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Recovery Time (ms)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Swap vs Recomputation: When to Use Which Strategy', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
ax.set_xlim(0, 1000)

# Add annotation
ax.annotate('Swap wins\n(small KV-cache)', xy=(100, 1), fontsize=10, color='green',
            fontweight='bold')
ax.annotate('Recompute wins\n(large KV-cache,\nshort prompt)', xy=(700, 10), fontsize=10, 
            color='red', fontweight='bold')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    # Visualize the crossover point fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6)) kv_sizes = np.linspace(1, 1000, 100) prompt_lens = [128, 512, 2048, 4096] colors = ['#3498db', '#2ecc71', '#e74c3c', '#9b59b6'] # Swap time is only dependent on KV-cache size swap_time = 2 * (kv_sizes / 1024) / 32.0 * 1000 # PCIe 4.0 x16 ax.plot(kv_sizes, swap_time, 'k--', linewidth=2, label='Swap (PCIe 4.0 x16)') for prompt_len, color in zip(prompt_lens, colors): recomp_time = np.full_like(kv_sizes, prompt_len / 10000 * 1000) ax.axhline(y=prompt_len / 10000 * 1000, color=color, alpha=0.7, linestyle='-', label=f'Recompute ({prompt_len} tokens)') ax.set_xlabel('KV-Cache Size (MB)', fontsize=12) ax.set_ylabel('Recovery Time (ms)', fontsize=12) ax.set_title('Swap vs Recomputation: When to Use Which Strategy', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold') ax.legend(fontsize=10) ax.grid(alpha=0.3) ax.set_xlim(0, 1000) # Add annotation ax.annotate('Swap wins\n(small KV-cache)', xy=(100, 1), fontsize=10, color='green', fontweight='bold') ax.annotate('Recompute wins\n(large KV-cache,\nshort prompt)', xy=(700, 10), fontsize=10, color='red', fontweight='bold') plt.tight_layout() plt.show()

    5. Preemptive Scheduler Implementation¶

    Let's implement a full preemptive scheduler that can evict running requests to make room for higher-priority ones.

    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    class PreemptiveScheduler:
    """Scheduler with preemption support (swap or recompute)."""
    
    def __init__(self, max_batch_size=4, total_gpu_blocks=100,
                 block_size=16, preemption_mode='swap'):
        self.max_batch_size = max_batch_size
        self.total_gpu_blocks = total_gpu_blocks
        self.block_size = block_size
        self.preemption_mode = preemption_mode
        
        self.waiting_queue = []
        self.running_batch = []
        self.preempted_queue = []
        self.completed = []
        self.free_blocks = total_gpu_blocks
        self.current_time = 0.0
        self.iteration_time = 0.025
        self.preemption_events = []
    
    def _blocks_needed(self, req):
        return (req.prompt_tokens + req.max_gen_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size
    
    def _priority(self, req):
        return req.priority * 100 + (self.current_time - req.arrival_time) * 10
    
    def add_request(self, req):
        req.state = RequestState.WAITING
        self.waiting_queue.append(req)
    
    def _try_preempt(self, needed_blocks: int, min_priority: float) -> List[SchedulerRequest]:
        """Try to preempt running requests to free up blocks.
        Returns list of preempted requests, or empty list if not possible."""
        # Sort running batch by priority (lowest first - preempt least important)
        candidates = sorted(self.running_batch, key=lambda r: self._priority(r))
        
        preempted = []
        freed = 0
        for req in candidates:
            if self._priority(req) >= min_priority:
                break  # Don't preempt requests with higher priority
            
            preempted.append(req)
            freed += req.kv_cache_blocks
            if freed >= needed_blocks:
                break
        
        if freed >= needed_blocks:
            return preempted
        return []  # Can't free enough
    
    def schedule(self):
        # Step 1: Complete finished requests
        newly_completed = []
        for req in self.running_batch:
            if req.generated_tokens >= req.max_gen_tokens:
                req.state = RequestState.COMPLETED
                req.completion_time = self.current_time
                self.free_blocks += req.kv_cache_blocks
                newly_completed.append(req)
        for req in newly_completed:
            self.running_batch.remove(req)
            self.completed.append(req)
        
        # Step 2: Try to admit preempted requests first (they already did work)
        readmitted = []
        still_preempted = []
        for req in sorted(self.preempted_queue, key=lambda r: self._priority(r), reverse=True):
            blocks = self._blocks_needed(req)
            if (len(self.running_batch) + len(readmitted) < self.max_batch_size and
                blocks <= self.free_blocks):
                req.state = RequestState.RUNNING
                req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks
                self.free_blocks -= blocks
                readmitted.append(req)
            else:
                still_preempted.append(req)
        self.preempted_queue = still_preempted
        self.running_batch.extend(readmitted)
        
        # Step 3: Admit new requests, with preemption if needed
        prefill_batch = []
        self.waiting_queue.sort(key=lambda r: self._priority(r), reverse=True)
        still_waiting = []
        
        for req in self.waiting_queue:
            if len(self.running_batch) + len(prefill_batch) >= self.max_batch_size:
                still_waiting.append(req)
                continue
            
            blocks = self._blocks_needed(req)
            if blocks <= self.free_blocks:
                req.state = RequestState.RUNNING
                req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks
                self.free_blocks -= blocks
                req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time
                prefill_batch.append(req)
            else:
                # Try preemption
                preemptable = self._try_preempt(blocks - self.free_blocks, self._priority(req))
                if preemptable:
                    for p in preemptable:
                        p.state = RequestState.PREEMPTED
                        p.total_preemptions += 1
                        self.free_blocks += p.kv_cache_blocks
                        self.running_batch.remove(p)
                        
                        if self.preemption_mode == 'recompute':
                            p.generated_tokens = 0  # Must redo all work
                        
                        self.preempted_queue.append(p)
                        self.preemption_events.append({
                            'time': self.current_time,
                            'preempted': p.request_id,
                            'by': req.request_id,
                            'mode': self.preemption_mode,
                        })
                    
                    req.state = RequestState.RUNNING
                    req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks
                    self.free_blocks -= blocks
                    req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time
                    prefill_batch.append(req)
                else:
                    still_waiting.append(req)
        
        self.waiting_queue = still_waiting
        self.running_batch.extend(prefill_batch)
        
        return {
            'time': self.current_time,
            'prefill': [r.request_id for r in prefill_batch],
            'decode': [r.request_id for r in self.running_batch if r not in prefill_batch],
            'completed': [r.request_id for r in newly_completed],
            'preempted': [e['preempted'] for e in self.preemption_events if e['time'] == self.current_time],
            'readmitted': [r.request_id for r in readmitted],
        }
    
    def step(self):
        decision = self.schedule()
        for req in self.running_batch:
            req.generated_tokens += 1
            if req.generated_tokens == 1:
                req.first_token_time = self.current_time
        self.current_time += self.iteration_time
        return decision

# Test preemptive scheduling
print("=== Preemptive Scheduling (Swap Mode) ===")
preemptive = PreemptiveScheduler(max_batch_size=3, total_gpu_blocks=50, 
                                  block_size=16, preemption_mode='swap')

# Low priority request arrives first and uses lots of memory
preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("LP1", 0.0, 128, 40, priority=1))
preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("LP2", 0.01, 64, 30, priority=1))
preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("LP3", 0.02, 96, 35, priority=1))

# Run a few steps
for _ in range(5):
    d = preemptive.step()

# High priority request arrives!
preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("HP1", preemptive.current_time, 64, 20, priority=10))
print(f"\n--- High-priority request HP1 arrives at t={preemptive.current_time:.3f}s ---\n")

for i in range(60):
    d = preemptive.step()
    if d['prefill'] or d['completed'] or d['preempted'] or d.get('readmitted'):
        parts = [f"t={d['time']:.3f}s:"]
        if d['preempted']: parts.append(f"PREEMPTED={d['preempted']}")
        if d['prefill']: parts.append(f"prefill={d['prefill']}")
        if d.get('readmitted'): parts.append(f"readmit={d['readmitted']}")
        if d['completed']: parts.append(f"completed={d['completed']}")
        print("  " + " | ".join(parts))
    if not preemptive.running_batch and not preemptive.waiting_queue and not preemptive.preempted_queue:
        break

print(f"\n=== Preemption Events ===")
for event in preemptive.preemption_events:
    print(f"  t={event['time']:.3f}s: {event['preempted']} preempted by {event['by']} ({event['mode']})")

print(f"\n=== Final Results ===")
for req in sorted(preemptive.completed, key=lambda r: r.request_id):
    ttft = (req.first_token_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000 if req.first_token_time else float('inf')
    total = (req.completion_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000
    print(f"  {req.request_id} (pri={req.priority}): TTFT={ttft:.0f}ms, Total={total:.0f}ms, "
          f"Preemptions={req.total_preemptions}")
    class PreemptiveScheduler: """Scheduler with preemption support (swap or recompute).""" def __init__(self, max_batch_size=4, total_gpu_blocks=100, block_size=16, preemption_mode='swap'): self.max_batch_size = max_batch_size self.total_gpu_blocks = total_gpu_blocks self.block_size = block_size self.preemption_mode = preemption_mode self.waiting_queue = [] self.running_batch = [] self.preempted_queue = [] self.completed = [] self.free_blocks = total_gpu_blocks self.current_time = 0.0 self.iteration_time = 0.025 self.preemption_events = [] def _blocks_needed(self, req): return (req.prompt_tokens + req.max_gen_tokens + self.block_size - 1) // self.block_size def _priority(self, req): return req.priority * 100 + (self.current_time - req.arrival_time) * 10 def add_request(self, req): req.state = RequestState.WAITING self.waiting_queue.append(req) def _try_preempt(self, needed_blocks: int, min_priority: float) -> List[SchedulerRequest]: """Try to preempt running requests to free up blocks. Returns list of preempted requests, or empty list if not possible.""" # Sort running batch by priority (lowest first - preempt least important) candidates = sorted(self.running_batch, key=lambda r: self._priority(r)) preempted = [] freed = 0 for req in candidates: if self._priority(req) >= min_priority: break # Don't preempt requests with higher priority preempted.append(req) freed += req.kv_cache_blocks if freed >= needed_blocks: break if freed >= needed_blocks: return preempted return [] # Can't free enough def schedule(self): # Step 1: Complete finished requests newly_completed = [] for req in self.running_batch: if req.generated_tokens >= req.max_gen_tokens: req.state = RequestState.COMPLETED req.completion_time = self.current_time self.free_blocks += req.kv_cache_blocks newly_completed.append(req) for req in newly_completed: self.running_batch.remove(req) self.completed.append(req) # Step 2: Try to admit preempted requests first (they already did work) readmitted = [] still_preempted = [] for req in sorted(self.preempted_queue, key=lambda r: self._priority(r), reverse=True): blocks = self._blocks_needed(req) if (len(self.running_batch) + len(readmitted) < self.max_batch_size and blocks <= self.free_blocks): req.state = RequestState.RUNNING req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks self.free_blocks -= blocks readmitted.append(req) else: still_preempted.append(req) self.preempted_queue = still_preempted self.running_batch.extend(readmitted) # Step 3: Admit new requests, with preemption if needed prefill_batch = [] self.waiting_queue.sort(key=lambda r: self._priority(r), reverse=True) still_waiting = [] for req in self.waiting_queue: if len(self.running_batch) + len(prefill_batch) >= self.max_batch_size: still_waiting.append(req) continue blocks = self._blocks_needed(req) if blocks <= self.free_blocks: req.state = RequestState.RUNNING req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks self.free_blocks -= blocks req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time prefill_batch.append(req) else: # Try preemption preemptable = self._try_preempt(blocks - self.free_blocks, self._priority(req)) if preemptable: for p in preemptable: p.state = RequestState.PREEMPTED p.total_preemptions += 1 self.free_blocks += p.kv_cache_blocks self.running_batch.remove(p) if self.preemption_mode == 'recompute': p.generated_tokens = 0 # Must redo all work self.preempted_queue.append(p) self.preemption_events.append({ 'time': self.current_time, 'preempted': p.request_id, 'by': req.request_id, 'mode': self.preemption_mode, }) req.state = RequestState.RUNNING req.kv_cache_blocks = blocks self.free_blocks -= blocks req.first_scheduled_time = self.current_time prefill_batch.append(req) else: still_waiting.append(req) self.waiting_queue = still_waiting self.running_batch.extend(prefill_batch) return { 'time': self.current_time, 'prefill': [r.request_id for r in prefill_batch], 'decode': [r.request_id for r in self.running_batch if r not in prefill_batch], 'completed': [r.request_id for r in newly_completed], 'preempted': [e['preempted'] for e in self.preemption_events if e['time'] == self.current_time], 'readmitted': [r.request_id for r in readmitted], } def step(self): decision = self.schedule() for req in self.running_batch: req.generated_tokens += 1 if req.generated_tokens == 1: req.first_token_time = self.current_time self.current_time += self.iteration_time return decision # Test preemptive scheduling print("=== Preemptive Scheduling (Swap Mode) ===") preemptive = PreemptiveScheduler(max_batch_size=3, total_gpu_blocks=50, block_size=16, preemption_mode='swap') # Low priority request arrives first and uses lots of memory preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("LP1", 0.0, 128, 40, priority=1)) preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("LP2", 0.01, 64, 30, priority=1)) preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("LP3", 0.02, 96, 35, priority=1)) # Run a few steps for _ in range(5): d = preemptive.step() # High priority request arrives! preemptive.add_request(SchedulerRequest("HP1", preemptive.current_time, 64, 20, priority=10)) print(f"\n--- High-priority request HP1 arrives at t={preemptive.current_time:.3f}s ---\n") for i in range(60): d = preemptive.step() if d['prefill'] or d['completed'] or d['preempted'] or d.get('readmitted'): parts = [f"t={d['time']:.3f}s:"] if d['preempted']: parts.append(f"PREEMPTED={d['preempted']}") if d['prefill']: parts.append(f"prefill={d['prefill']}") if d.get('readmitted'): parts.append(f"readmit={d['readmitted']}") if d['completed']: parts.append(f"completed={d['completed']}") print(" " + " | ".join(parts)) if not preemptive.running_batch and not preemptive.waiting_queue and not preemptive.preempted_queue: break print(f"\n=== Preemption Events ===") for event in preemptive.preemption_events: print(f" t={event['time']:.3f}s: {event['preempted']} preempted by {event['by']} ({event['mode']})") print(f"\n=== Final Results ===") for req in sorted(preemptive.completed, key=lambda r: r.request_id): ttft = (req.first_token_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000 if req.first_token_time else float('inf') total = (req.completion_time - req.arrival_time) * 1000 print(f" {req.request_id} (pri={req.priority}): TTFT={ttft:.0f}ms, Total={total:.0f}ms, " f"Preemptions={req.total_preemptions}")

    6. Admission Control: Scheduling with Limited GPU Memory¶

    GPU memory is the most precious resource in LLM serving. The scheduler must perform admission control to prevent out-of-memory errors.

    Memory Budget Components¶

    1. Model weights: Fixed cost, loaded once
    2. KV-cache: Variable, proportional to (batch_size * seq_len * num_layers * hidden_dim)
    3. Activation memory: Variable, needed during forward pass
    4. CUDA overhead: Driver allocations, fragmentation

    KV-Cache Memory Formula¶

    For a single request with sequence length $s$:

    $$\text{KV\_cache\_bytes} = 2 \times n_{\text{layers}} \times s \times d_{\text{model}} \times \text{sizeof}(\text{dtype})$$

    For Llama-3-8B (32 layers, 4096 hidden, FP16):

    • Per token: $2 \times 32 \times 4096 \times 2 = 524,288$ bytes = 0.5 MB per token!
    • A 4096-token sequence: ~2 GB of KV-cache
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    # KV-Cache Memory Calculator

def kv_cache_memory(num_layers, hidden_dim, seq_len, dtype_bytes=2, 
                     num_kv_heads=None):
    """Calculate KV-cache memory for one request.
    
    If num_kv_heads is set (GQA), uses that instead of full hidden_dim.
    """
    if num_kv_heads:
        head_dim = hidden_dim // 32  # assuming 32 total heads
        kv_dim = num_kv_heads * head_dim
    else:
        kv_dim = hidden_dim
    
    # 2 for K and V
    return 2 * num_layers * seq_len * kv_dim * dtype_bytes

# Calculate for popular models
models = [
    ("Llama-3-8B", 32, 4096, None, 8192),
    ("Llama-3-70B", 80, 8192, 8, 8192),  # GQA with 8 KV heads
    ("Llama-3-405B", 126, 16384, 8, 131072),
    ("Qwen2.5-72B", 80, 8192, 8, 131072),
]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

# Plot 1: KV-cache per token
ax = axes[0]
model_names = []
per_token_mb = []

print("=== KV-Cache Memory Analysis ===")
print(f"{'Model':<20} {'Per Token (KB)':<16} {'1K tokens (MB)':<16} {'4K tokens (MB)':<16} {'Max Context (GB)':<16}")
print("-" * 84)

for name, layers, hidden, kv_heads, max_ctx in models:
    mem_1 = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, 1, num_kv_heads=kv_heads)
    mem_1k = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, 1024, num_kv_heads=kv_heads)
    mem_4k = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, 4096, num_kv_heads=kv_heads)
    mem_max = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, max_ctx, num_kv_heads=kv_heads)
    
    model_names.append(name)
    per_token_mb.append(mem_1 / 1024)
    
    print(f"{name:<20} {mem_1/1024:<16.1f} {mem_1k/1024/1024:<16.1f} "
          f"{mem_4k/1024/1024:<16.1f} {mem_max/1024/1024/1024:<16.2f}")

ax.bar(model_names, per_token_mb, color=['#3498db', '#e74c3c', '#2ecc71', '#f39c12'], alpha=0.8)
ax.set_ylabel('KV-Cache per Token (KB)')
ax.set_title('KV-Cache Memory per Token by Model', fontweight='bold')
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.setp(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=15)

# Plot 2: How many concurrent requests fit?
ax = axes[1]
gpu_memory_gb = 80  # A100-80GB
model_weight_gb = [16, 140, 810, 144]  # approximate FP16 weights

for i, (name, layers, hidden, kv_heads, max_ctx) in enumerate(models):
    if model_weight_gb[i] > gpu_memory_gb:
        continue  # Skip models that don't fit on single GPU
    
    available_gb = gpu_memory_gb - model_weight_gb[i] - 2  # 2GB overhead
    seq_lens = np.arange(128, min(max_ctx, 8192), 128)
    max_concurrent = []
    for sl in seq_lens:
        mem_per_req = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, sl, num_kv_heads=kv_heads)
        max_concurrent.append(available_gb * 1024**3 / mem_per_req)
    
    ax.plot(seq_lens, max_concurrent, '-', label=name, linewidth=2)

ax.set_xlabel('Sequence Length (tokens)')
ax.set_ylabel('Max Concurrent Requests')
ax.set_title(f'Max Concurrent Requests on A100-80GB', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
ax.set_ylim(0)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
    # KV-Cache Memory Calculator def kv_cache_memory(num_layers, hidden_dim, seq_len, dtype_bytes=2, num_kv_heads=None): """Calculate KV-cache memory for one request. If num_kv_heads is set (GQA), uses that instead of full hidden_dim. """ if num_kv_heads: head_dim = hidden_dim // 32 # assuming 32 total heads kv_dim = num_kv_heads * head_dim else: kv_dim = hidden_dim # 2 for K and V return 2 * num_layers * seq_len * kv_dim * dtype_bytes # Calculate for popular models models = [ ("Llama-3-8B", 32, 4096, None, 8192), ("Llama-3-70B", 80, 8192, 8, 8192), # GQA with 8 KV heads ("Llama-3-405B", 126, 16384, 8, 131072), ("Qwen2.5-72B", 80, 8192, 8, 131072), ] fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5)) # Plot 1: KV-cache per token ax = axes[0] model_names = [] per_token_mb = [] print("=== KV-Cache Memory Analysis ===") print(f"{'Model':<20} {'Per Token (KB)':<16} {'1K tokens (MB)':<16} {'4K tokens (MB)':<16} {'Max Context (GB)':<16}") print("-" * 84) for name, layers, hidden, kv_heads, max_ctx in models: mem_1 = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, 1, num_kv_heads=kv_heads) mem_1k = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, 1024, num_kv_heads=kv_heads) mem_4k = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, 4096, num_kv_heads=kv_heads) mem_max = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, max_ctx, num_kv_heads=kv_heads) model_names.append(name) per_token_mb.append(mem_1 / 1024) print(f"{name:<20} {mem_1/1024:<16.1f} {mem_1k/1024/1024:<16.1f} " f"{mem_4k/1024/1024:<16.1f} {mem_max/1024/1024/1024:<16.2f}") ax.bar(model_names, per_token_mb, color=['#3498db', '#e74c3c', '#2ecc71', '#f39c12'], alpha=0.8) ax.set_ylabel('KV-Cache per Token (KB)') ax.set_title('KV-Cache Memory per Token by Model', fontweight='bold') ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3) plt.setp(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=15) # Plot 2: How many concurrent requests fit? ax = axes[1] gpu_memory_gb = 80 # A100-80GB model_weight_gb = [16, 140, 810, 144] # approximate FP16 weights for i, (name, layers, hidden, kv_heads, max_ctx) in enumerate(models): if model_weight_gb[i] > gpu_memory_gb: continue # Skip models that don't fit on single GPU available_gb = gpu_memory_gb - model_weight_gb[i] - 2 # 2GB overhead seq_lens = np.arange(128, min(max_ctx, 8192), 128) max_concurrent = [] for sl in seq_lens: mem_per_req = kv_cache_memory(layers, hidden, sl, num_kv_heads=kv_heads) max_concurrent.append(available_gb * 1024**3 / mem_per_req) ax.plot(seq_lens, max_concurrent, '-', label=name, linewidth=2) ax.set_xlabel('Sequence Length (tokens)') ax.set_ylabel('Max Concurrent Requests') ax.set_title(f'Max Concurrent Requests on A100-80GB', fontweight='bold') ax.legend() ax.grid(alpha=0.3) ax.set_ylim(0) plt.tight_layout() plt.show()

    7. Fairness vs Efficiency Tradeoff¶

    A fundamental tension in scheduler design:

    • Fairness: Every request should get reasonable service, regardless of priority
    • Efficiency: Maximize GPU utilization and total throughput
    • SLO compliance: Meet latency guarantees for paying customers

    These goals often conflict:

    • Batching more requests improves throughput but increases per-request latency
    • Prioritizing high-priority requests may starve low-priority ones
    • Preemption recovers resources but wastes the work already done

    Strategies to Balance¶

    1. Aging: Increase priority of waiting requests over time to prevent starvation
    2. Weighted fair queueing: Allocate GPU time proportionally to priority weights
    3. Reservation: Reserve some batch slots for each priority tier
    4. Deadline-aware: Only preempt when SLO violation is imminent
    In [ ]:
    Copied! 
    # Compare FCFS vs Priority vs Preemptive scheduling

def run_simulation(scheduler_class, requests_data, **kwargs):
    """Run a scheduling simulation and return metrics."""
    sched = scheduler_class(**kwargs)
    
    # Add all requests
    requests = []
    for r in requests_data:
        req = SchedulerRequest(**r)
        requests.append(req)
        sched.add_request(req)
    
    # Run until all complete
    for _ in range(500):
        sched.step()
        has_work = bool(sched.running_batch) or bool(sched.waiting_queue)
        if hasattr(sched, 'preempted_queue'):
